description: Pure-Swift, dependency-free triangle-mesh healing: broken / non-watertight STL bodies into closed manifold solids on iOS + macOS. Liepa hole filling, generalized winding number, feature-preserving.
rules: Import with `import SwiftMeshHeal`. The only public type is `MeshHeal`, a Sendable/Equatable value type; there is no dependency (no OCCT, CGAL, GMP, Eigen) and no native build step.
Construct with `MeshHeal(positions:indices:)` — `positions` is `[SIMD3<Float>]` welded vertices, `indices` is a flat `[UInt32]` buffer of 3 vertex indices per triangle.
`tier1Healed(skipLoop:)` is THE entry point: it removes membranes/duplicates, resolves non-manifold edges, and Liepa-fills holes, returning `(mesh, filled, skipped, remainingUnfilled)`.
Operations are non-mutating and return a new `MeshHeal`: `removingDuplicateFaces()`, `resolveNonManifoldEdges()`, `repairedManifold(maxFaces:)`. The receiver is never changed (value semantics).
Inspect state with the properties `isWatertight`, `nonManifoldEdgeCount`, `enclosedVolume`, `surfaceArea`, `pcaExtents`, `bounds`, `isDegenerateSheet`, and the method `boundaryLoops()`.

By default every boundary loop is filled (a missing back is a defect, not an opening). Protect genuine through-openings (windows/doors) by passing `throughOpeningSkip(minArea:clearDist:)` as `skipLoop`.
Lower-level filling: `liepaFill(loop:bndNormals:existingEdges:)` (min-dihedral patch, returns nil for slits), `boundaryEdgeFaceNormals()`, and `filledHoles(skipLoop:)`.
Geometry primitives: `generalizedWindingNumber(_:)` (~1 inside, ~0 outside) and `firstRayHit(origin:direction:maxDist:eps:)` (Moller-Trumbore, returns distance or nil).
